evidence_quotes.quote: Video Games are not just very important to me and many other people as a hobby - but they are also a form of art - art that has helped me (and others) change.
I want Video Games to be protected - because it would suck if someone was robbed of these experiences because companies decided to shut it down.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I'd like to ask if it is possible to include Video Games into the digital fairness act. Video Games are not just very important to me and many other people as a hobby - but they are also a form of art - art that has helped me (and others) change. The Kingdom Hearts and the Persona series have really impacted me as a person and have changed the way I view certain things, without them I'd probably be a different person than who I am right now. That is why I want Video Games to be protected - because it would suck if someone was robbed of these experiences because companies decided to shut it down. In the "Stop Killing Games" wiki there is a list of games that are either dead, or at risk - if I were a massive fan of these games I'd be devastated to know that these franchises that I have enjoyed so much disappear, and there probably is someone like that out there who found out their favourite game is being shut down. I want to prevent this from happening, even if just typing this out helps a tiny bit - I am really passionate about video games and I don't want to see the Video Game industry cannibalize their own games for profit. Thanks for reading.
